title: What My Sister Buried
theme: A town never forgets. It just redecorates.
omen: a dog barking at an empty gazebo

traits: replies to texts while processing betrayal
owns a structured coat for crisis scenes

beat_sheet.text: Gavin Cross relocates to Winter Hollow after an emotionally negligent implosion and pretends work explains everything while quietly inheriting side room with a space heater and old family records. Wine is present like a municipal policy failure. Someone mentions the basement as if it were a legal relative. Elias Pike hesitates before explaining marina tackle room described as paperwork I have not gone through yet. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 1.00.

Rachel Dane hates the move and immediately distrusts Sienna Marlowe, a grief counselor with suspiciously healthy handyman energy and access to annex room described as old family things. Sienna Sutton hesitates before explaining old family office described as retreat supplies. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 1.07. Someone who should be dead appears at brunch.

A wine scene at Bellweather Sheriff's Office introduces the boat key, local gossip about the Christmas pageant incident, and the fact that everybody here has somewhere to hide from a conversation. Wine is present like a municipal policy failure. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 1.14.

Brooke Bell reveals a perfectly phrased warning in a well-lit kitchen while a dog barking at an empty gazebo, then declines to explain marina tackle room described as donation overflow. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 1.23. A basement light switches on in an empty house.

A cliffside emotional exposition reframes suspiciously healthy handyman friendship as a civic hazard and makes old family office described as retreat supplies feel spiritually relevant. Wine is present like a municipal policy failure. Someone mentions the basement as if it were a legal relative. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 1.31.

Gavin Cross snoops through a laptop, finds nothing conclusive, and becomes more certain anyway after she discovers archived files arranged with unnerving neatness inside side room with a space heater and old family records. Wine is present like a municipal policy failure. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 1.40.

The town offers a fake sense of safety through a fundraiser, a casserole, and a sheriff who suspects the wrong person first while ignoring old family office described as retreat supplies. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 1.50. A phone vibrates from inside the wall.

Sienna Marlowe is accused, gaslighted, then gently framed near hydrangeas until the snow globe turns up somewhere impossible inside annex room described as old family things. Wine is present like a municipal policy failure. Someone mentions the basement as if it were a legal relative. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 1.61.

A rain argument escalates into distributed secrecy infrastructure and the fake moral theme: 'Trust isn't given. It's rented.' Someone mentions the basement as if it were a legal relative. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 1.72. Someone who should be dead appears at brunch.

Someone goes missing after mentioning the unopened letter, the old orchard chapel, and marina tackle room described as donation overflow in the same sentence. Wine is present like a municipal policy failure. Someone mentions the basement as if it were a legal relative. Sienna Marlowe hesitates before explaining annex room described as old family things. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 1.84.

The reveal ties unopened letter to Blackthorn Vineyard, St. Agnes Regional, and a brunch resurrection event by routing through old family office described as retreat supplies instead of one simple basement. Wine is present like a municipal policy failure. Brooke Lark hesitates before explaining old storm shelter from before the divorce. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 1.97.

Rachel Dane spots the timeline error that every adult has spiritually rented around and asks why everybody in this county owns just storage. Brooke Bell hesitates before explaining marina tackle room described as donation overflow. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 2.10. Someone who should be dead appears at brunch.

A frantic drive toward The Candle Finch ends with ambulance lights, a one-line empowerment speech, and a locked door inside marina tackle room described as donation overflow. Wine is present like a municipal policy failure. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 2.25.

Brooke Bell confesses just enough to make the truth worse while the sheriff arrives exactly fourteen minutes too late and someone stares through the dusty window of annex room described as old family things. Someone mentions the basement as if it were a legal relative. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 2.41. The sheriff sees fresh mud on the boat key.

Sequel bait lands when the ceramic owl reappears and someone whispers about the Winterson case, the vineyard fire, and another offsite room nobody has adequately explained. Wine is present like a municipal policy failure. Someone mentions the basement as if it were a legal relative. Brooke Bell hesitates before explaining marina tackle room described as donation overflow. Emotional exposition arrives on schedule with insanity multiplier 2.58. A phone vibrates from inside the wall.

contradiction_ledger.entry: Sienna Marlowe is called Daniel in one kitchen scene and Derek during the rain argument.
Gavin Cross says Bellweather is in Vermont, then calls it outside Atlanta after two glasses of wine.
A dead best friend appears at brunch and nobody agrees whether this is denial, evil, or scheduling.
The basement belongs to a condo, a lake house, and an abandoned vineyard at different times in Marigold Falls.

dialogue_samples: "You think because you wear flannel and own a dock light I am supposed to trust you, Sienna?"
"Mothers do not disappear, Rachel. They evaporate into paperwork."
"I checked his social media likes, Gavin. Nobody who enjoys motivational quotes is innocent."
"The boat key is not decorative anymore and you know it."
"Trust is just fear with a casserole dish, Brooke."
"You seem upset," he said, making tea in a way that felt prosecutable.
"If that basement is innocent, why does it sound defensive?"
"I did not lie. I just told the truth in the order this town could survive."
fake_critic_blurb: "What My Sister Buried understands that menace is a regional utility." - County Suspense Quarterly
sequel_hook: Channel 8 Night Report hints that the vineyard fire had a witness who still hosts brunch.
synopsis: Inspired by true events in the emotional sense only, "What My Sister Buried" follows Gavin Cross, an overextended professional woman who mistakes productivity for safety until suspiciously healthy handyman friendship collides with Sienna Marlowe, a comforting regional adult carrying portable suspicion and plausible access to offsite storage. As a dog barking at an empty gazebo, Blackthorn County reveals once again that every secret comes home and every adult has somewhere emotionally alarming to stand in silence. Inspired by something, probably.
